overview.phase_description: Email Triage is live and hardened, delivering Chuck a single prioritized action list every morning at 5:30 AM ET. The Live Scheduler is the active build: a day-level crew board that reads the team's Outlook calendars, shows which crews are open and qualified for each job, flags travel distance to the site, and lets Jovani reschedule in one move. It is now connected to the live calendar and confirmed to read the full week of crew assignments accurately. Next is a working review with Chuck and Jovani on June 30, then two polished versions to present to Ron in September. Google Ads continue in Jacksonville, and the latest Local Pack rescan confirms Tampa dominance (17 of 21 keywords ranked #1-2).
overview.tagline: Your AI Business Operating System — Designed, Installed, Managed.

quick_wins.title: Email Triage System
quick_wins.description: Chuck's Outlook (chuck@aslp.net) — 50+ emails daily. Categorizes into Action Required / FYI / Bulk Noise, delivers a single prioritized daily digest at 5:45 AM ET. LIVE April 30, 2026. Week 6 review (May 12) identified 3 refinements: surface emails from non-primary folders, replace number IDs with sender/subject, add direct email links. v2 deployed this week — adds clickable 'Open Email' buttons, identifies by sender/subject, shows source folder, auto-moves action items to 'Action Items' folder in Outlook.
quick_wins.impact: Eliminates 1–2 hours of daily inbox paralysis. Delivers one clear action list every morning before the crew heads out. Action items automatically moved to dedicated folder for immediate access.

quick_wins.title: AI Data Center Lightning Protection Campaign
quick_wins.description: Dedicated service page + Google keyword campaign targeting AI data center construction — an exploding market issuing lightning protection contracts years in advance. Current competition on Google Ads is minimal, making traffic inexpensive to capture now. Chuck flagged this as URGENT in Week 6 session.
quick_wins.impact: Positions All South as the go-to specialist for AI infrastructure lightning protection at the start of a multi-year construction boom.

quick_wins.title: Phone Call Transcription
quick_wins.description: Calls transcribed automatically → organized into lead file → moves to job file. Solves costly disputes when customers claim job details differ from agreement. Otter removed for being too aggressive.
quick_wins.impact: Complete call-to-job record. Eliminates 'he said she said' disputes.

quick_wins.title: Live Scheduler — Jovani
quick_wins.description: A day-level crew board for Tampa that reads the team's Outlook calendars and lets Jovani reschedule in one move. It matches each job to a qualified, available crew, shows distance from the site, and keeps the calendar as the single source of truth. Now connected to the live calendar and reading the full week of crew assignments accurately. Intake via PM and estimators; client notifications triggered by the estimator. Five Tampa crews (foreman + worker each). Target: two builds to present to Ron by September.
quick_wins.impact: Transforms 'I can't do that' into 'Here are your options.' Stops revenue loss from poor scheduling decisions. Restores customer confidence. Brain gets used daily without Jovani ever thinking about it.

quick_wins.title: Project Template Automation
quick_wins.description: Chuck identified a need to standardize how job requirements are communicated to clients and subcontractors at the start of each project. Template system to cover job phases, staging requirements, and subcontractor prep — delivered automatically when job is assigned. Saves ~5 hrs/week. Next step: connect template to Trimble job details for auto-delivery.
quick_wins.impact: Eliminates manual communication overhead, educates subcontractors without on-site supervision, reduces rescheduling from unprepared sites.

quick_wins.title: Field Crew Photo Gate
quick_wins.description: Missing photo evidence can require removing concrete to prove work was done. Proposed: a gate system that blocks timesheet submission until required photos are uploaded.
quick_wins.impact: Eliminates legal/cost exposure from missing job site documentation.

quick_wins.title: Customer Retention Touchpoint
quick_wins.description: Monday.com annual email ineffective — GC hires don't share building owner contact. QR sticker on panels is workaround but not in closeout checklist. Need: sticker added to mandatory closeout gate + tracking system.
quick_wins.impact: Grows inspection department — long-term high-margin revenue stream.

quick_wins.title: Automated Quote Analysis
quick_wins.description: Jeff manually reviews up to 1,000 pages of architect drawings and spec books per quote. AI system could digitally process documents and extract required data to dramatically accelerate quoting.
quick_wins.impact: Saves hours per quote across thousands of jobs per year. Massive capacity unlock.

sessions.label: Week 1: Full Business Discovery
topics: Business overview — what All South does and day-to-day operations
Revenue model — $13M projected 2026, 90% repeat business, avg $20-30K contract
Employee structure — ~300 total across 3 locations (Tampa HQ, Sunrise/Miami, Jacksonville)
Training program — AI video series built by Chuck to eliminate telephone effect; OSHA 10; retraining older crew now
SOP & documentation inventory — full form library, new hire manual; all policies need attorney review
Technology stack — Trimble suite (mid-transition), monday.com being retired, ChatGPT daily, Otter tried/removed
Key pain points — information siloing, field crew documentation, 30-50 emails/day, retention gap
Retention system — annual inspection automation being decommissioned, no replacement identified
Redundancy & succession — double-trained system; newer PMs receiving added support
Revenue targets — $13M 2026; $15-20M 3yr; $50M long-term vision
Next steps — Thursday recurring meeting, contract + invoice to be sent, ZooTampa press release

sessions.label: Week 2: Business Process Mapping
topics: SOP inventory — confirmed existing policies: accident reporting, vehicle use, cell phone, attendance/PTO, GPS, certifications roadmap, equipment sign-out, PM performance evals
Gaps identified: field crew photo SOP (unwritten), QR sticker not in mandatory closeout checklist
Server organization — Tampa only, branches excluded. Some docs on personal devices. Centralization is priority.
Lead intake mapped — Joe routes calls to Jeff/Morgan/Ron. Charley + Jovani also receive leads directly via text/email.
Quoting process — Jeff reviews 1,000-page document packages manually. 1-week turnaround. AI opportunity flagged.
Retention system — Monday.com email ineffective (no building owner contact from GC hires). QR sticker workaround in place.
Scheduling gap — high request volume in Outlook makes some requests hard to accommodate. Smart scheduler requested.
Automation pick confirmed: Email triage (#1) — 50 emails/day, 254 unread, wants daily actionable checklist
Call transcription confirmed as #2 priority
Daily brief / hub concept validated
Expo commercial video — visuals needed in Dropbox. Silent loop or captioned voiceover for trade show environment.
Next session: Thursday April 13 — Brain demo + expo video planning. Ron included in future website sessions.

sessions.label: Week 3: Business Brain Launch
topics: Business Brain launched at brain.allsouthlightningprotection.com
Secured with auto-renewing HTTPS encryption
Chuck and Morgan accounts created with private access
Brain loaded with business context from Weeks 1 and 2
Written recap and access instructions sent to Chuck

sessions.label: Week 4: Brain Demo + Email Triage Preview
topics: Business Brain used live for the first time — Chuck logged in and asked Day 1 question
Brain surfaced structural vulnerabilities: personnel backup gaps (Angie), SOP delivery gaps
Security briefing delivered — private server, NicheFinders owns infra, access control explained
Email triage specs confirmed: chuck@aslp.net, 6am delivery, Action/FYI/Noise categories, single prioritized list
Crystal identified as second triage inbox after Chuck
Morgan's role clarified: tech-forward, will be eager Brain user, point person for architectural AI workflow
Estimating workflow — route AI-assisted prep through Morgan, deliver near-complete files to Jeff for final review
SOP urgency elevated: paper-only delivery is legal and continuity risk
Code book brain concept discussed — Chuck's idea for 4 company code books
Trade show confirmed April 28 (setup April 27) — commercial video deadline URGENT
Obsidian visual dashboard discussed — to identify and manage workflow gaps

sessions.label: Week 5: Email Triage Live + Trade Show Video Delivered
topics: Email triage system demo — digest format reviewed with Chuck
Trade show commercial video delivered — ready for April 28 Tampa expo setup
Brain update — 164 knowledge chunks, 49 staff on record
Crystal Balog email triage — Phase 2 planned after Chuck's triage goes live

sessions.label: Week 6: Email Triage Review + Strategic Planning
topics: Email triage review — 3 refinements identified: surface emails not yet located, replace number IDs with sender/subject ('email 8' means nothing), add direct links to the original email
Action Items folder request — Chuck wants ACTION REQUIRED emails auto-moved to a dedicated Outlook folder
Brain v2 deployed May 13 — speed issue resolved. Rebuilt on dedicated infrastructure with upgraded search and ranking. No more long delays. Supports several users at once.
AI data center campaign — URGENT; Chuck pushed hard; dedicated service page + Google Ads campaign approved
Website content strategy — 2 services/month commitment; start with services list review + FAQ outlines
Brain → website FAQ integration concept — users ask questions; if Brain can't answer, connect to office staff; needs further design planning
Employee portal design — links hub (Paychex, 401k) + gated training; no complex financial infrastructure needed
Javanni scheduling pain — flagged as urgent AI priority for near future; spending half her day on constantly changing client demands
Jacksonville branch — confirmed as next major focus after triage is solid; ready for build-up, underperforming vs Tampa
Trimble image API — Chuck has emailed Crystal; limited access to job pictures only; vendor API cost vs seat to be researched
Visual agent + image pipeline — 75% self-images / 25% AI content target; Faraday + TVSS images to be categorized
ASLP.NET domain issue flagged as important — ~100,000 building maintenance tags reference this domain; being addressed with the client's IT
Payment status — check not received; Chuck to follow up with Angie; auto-debit setup planned

sessions.label: Week 7: Email Triage V3 Architecture Lock
topics: Email triage V3 approach locked — time-based lookback, automatic Action Items folder handling, sender-based rules plus smart judgment for external senders
V3 scheduled: 5:45 AM daily, 24hr lookback, digest to chuck@aslp.net
Email delivery domain configured for reliable inbox delivery of the daily digest
Internal email routing confirmed: Ron/Crystal/Angie/Javanni = Action Required; Jeff/Morgan/Jordan/Foremen = FYI
Chuck confirmed Action Items folder created in Outlook — replaces Jobs Folder at same tier
Brain speed upgrade confirmed: Chuck reported significantly improved answer retrieval speed
Coordinating with the client's IT to finalize secure email access permissions

sessions.label: Week 8/9: Press Release Verification + Hospital List Confirmed
topics: Healthcare press release: 4 discrepancies resolved live with Chuck
Hospital count updated 14 → 16 (Shriners Children's added)
Featured projects confirmed: AdventHealth Riverview (APG) + Moffitt Cancer Center Speros Branch (APG)
Miller Electric = confirmed official partnership
Remaining open items: Ron quote, legal hospital-naming sign-off, distribution channel decision

sessions.label: Week 10: Google Ads Jacksonville Launch + Local Pack Rescan
topics: 2 Search campaigns launched — Jacksonville DMA. $18/day total. Manual CPC. Mon–Fri 8am–5pm.
Campaign 1: Commercial & Data Center (4 ad groups). Campaign 2: Residential (1 ad group).
All RSAs: 15 headlines, 4 descriptions, Jacksonville-specific. Excellent ad strength target.
Local Pack rescan June 5: 17/21 Tampa keywords #1–2. FL LP code #3.26 → #1.01 (big win).
Regressions flagged: Lightning rod FL #1.26 → #3.20, LP cost Local Finder #1.45 → #7.17
Local_Pack_Scan_Jun2026.html delivered to portal — March vs. June comparison + gap action items

sessions.label: Week 11: Scheduling Discovery + Jovani OAuth Live
topics: Confirmed Javanni (Jovani) = Tampa scheduler only. Calendar access established.
All crew confirmed: individual @aslp.net Outlook calendars — full crew calendar access path viable
New job trigger confirmed: email from Ron/Jeff/Morgan + Trimble assignment
Crew tier matrix locked: Hayden=hospital/any, Nathaniel=govt, Roberto=large commercial/any, Josh/Nate/Adam=simple
Root causes of rescheduling: site not ready (#1), safety/partial install conflicts (#2), double bookings (#3)
APM (Assistant Project Manager) role being deployed for pre-arrival site checks
Template communication system in development — saves ~5 hrs/week once live
Client IT confirmed the account access permissions needed on this call
Smart Scheduler build unblocked — starting Week 11
Action item: Invite Roberto to next meeting to discuss his role

sessions.label: Week 12: Live Scheduler Demo + Triage Link-Fix
topics: Live Scheduler mock demoed — approval queue + crew availability board (1-click reschedule).
Decision: Live Scheduler becomes the PRIMARY data tool, replacing manual calendar management.
Decision: APM site-readiness checks stay MANUAL — excluded from the automated scheduler.
Confirmed crew structure: 5 crews, each a foreman + 1 worker, each on own Outlook calendar.
Intake = PM + estimators; client-notify email triggered by the estimator.
New requirements: intra-day time slots / split-shifts + distance-from-site in approvals.
Email Triage Open/Reply link mismatch fixed and deployed live.
Target: two Live Scheduler builds to present to Ron by September (AIOS evaluation).

upcoming_session.label: Week 13: Live Scheduler Review with Chuck + Jovani
agenda: Walkthrough of the working day-level crew board, reading the live Outlook calendars
Confirm the five Tampa crews and their crew pairings
Crew skills, clearances, and travel-distance setup
Day-level scheduling vs. intra-day time slots
Path to the September demo for Ron (two version builds)
